mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations, directly compressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 1.5% signals collapsing consumer demand, which forces corporate earnings revisions downward and drives negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.03 during high-volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous unemployment rate declines and payroll employment gains confirm labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending expectations and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expansion during a monetary easing cycle reflects improving supply-side capacity and corporate output, triggering short-term positive market repricing.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns in >55% of easing periods, or the mechanism reverses direction during tightening phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 240 trigger aggressive central bank tightening expectations, compressing equity multiples and producing negative forward market returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly gross domestic product growth reflects robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue trajectories and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no correlation with 60-day forward returns with Rank IC < 0.01, or positive GDP prints precede negative market moves in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with falling housing starts indicates deteriorating household balance sheets and weakening economic momentum, forecasting negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative returns in <45% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines during a monetary tightening regime reflect manufacturing stress and reduced capacity utilization, forecasting negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns in >55% of tightening periods, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation expectations are anchored.
